medicines.precautions: Can worsen narrow-angle glaucoma or urinary retention

medicines.name: Ipratropium (Atrovent)
medicines.use: Relieves acute bronchospasm in COPD
medicines.precautions: Avoid getting spray in eyes (can cause blurred vision or pupil dilation)

medicines.name: Albuterol/Ipratropium (Combivent, DuoNeb)
medicines.use: Relieves acute bronchospasm in COPD
medicines.precautions: Combines two mechanisms for better airway opening; monitor heart rate

medicines.name: Theophylline (Theo-24)
medicines.use: Maintenance treatment for asthma and COPD
medicines.precautions: Very narrow therapeutic index; requires blood level monitoring; toxicity causes seizures and arrhythmias

medicines.name: Roflumilast (Daliresp)
medicines.use: Reduces exacerbations in severe COPD
medicines.precautions: Causes significant weight loss, diarrhea, and psychiatric events

medicines.name: Zafirlukast (Accolate)
medicines.use: Prevents asthma attacks
medicines.precautions: Take on an empty stomach (1 hour before or 2 hours after meals); can cause liver toxicity

medicines.name: Zileuton (Zyflo)
medicines.use: Prevents asthma attacks
medicines.dosage_guidance: 600mg four times a day or twice daily (CR formulation)
medicines.precautions: Can cause liver toxicity; must monitor liver enzymes regularly

medicines.name: Omalizumab (Xolair)
medicines.use: Treats moderate to severe allergic asthma and chronic hives
medicines.precautions: Risk of severe allergic reaction (anaphylaxis); must carry an EpiPen

medicines.name: Mepolizumab (Nucala)
medicines.use: Treats severe eosinophilic asthma
medicines.precautions: Can cause headache and injection site reactions; treats a specific asthma subtype

medicines.name: Benralizumab (Fasenra)
medicines.use: Treats severe eosinophilic asthma
medicines.precautions: Less frequent dosing than mepolizumab; may cause headache or sore throat

medicines.name: Dupilumab (Dupixent)
medicines.use: Treats moderate to severe eczema, asthma, and nasal polyps
medicines.precautions: Can cause eye problems like conjunctivitis or keratitis

medicines.name: Carisoprodol (Soma)
medicines.use: Short-term treatment of acute muscle spasms
medicines.dosage_guidance: 250mg-350mg three times daily and at bedtime
medicines.precautions: High potential for abuse and dependence; avoid mixing with other CNS depressants

medicines.name: Orphenadrine (Norflex)
medicines.use: Treats muscle spasms and Parkinson's disease
medicines.precautions: Has strong anticholinergic side effects (dry mouth, blurred vision, urinary retention)

medicines.name: Metaxalone (Skelaxin)
medicines.use: Treats acute muscle spasms
medicines.precautions: Take with food to increase absorption; may cause nausea and drowsiness

medicines.name: Chlorzoxazone (Lorzone)
medicines.use: Treats muscle spasms and pain
medicines.precautions: May turn urine orange or reddish-purple; can cause liver toxicity

medicines.name: Aspirin/Acetaminophen/Caffeine (Excedrin)
medicines.use: Treats migraine and tension headaches
medicines.precautions: Contains significant caffeine; avoid taking near bedtime; watch for stomach bleeding from aspirin

medicines.name: Butalbital/Acetaminophen/Caffeine (Fioricet)
medicines.use: Treats tension headaches
medicines.precautions: High risk of rebound headaches and dependence if used frequently

medicines.name: Rizatriptan (Maxalt)
medicines.use: Treats acute migraine attacks
medicines.precautions: Fast acting but causes chest/throat tightness; contraindicated in ischemic heart disease

medicines.name: Zolmitriptan (Zomig)
medicines.use: Treats acute migraine attacks
medicines.precautions: Available as a nasal spray for rapid onset; causes tingling and dizziness

medicines.name: Eletriptan (Relpax)
medicines.use: Treats acute migraine attacks
medicines.precautions: Do not use within 72 hours of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors (e.g., clarithromycin, ketoconazole)

medicines.name: Naratriptan (Amerge)
medicines.use: Treats acute migraine attacks
medicines.precautions: Slower onset but lasts longer than other triptans, useful for long-lasting migraines

medicines.name: Frovatriptan (Frova)
medicines.use: Treats acute migraine attacks and prevents menstrual migraines
medicines.precautions: Has the longest half-life of all triptans; good for preventing rebound headaches

medicines.name: Ubrogepant (Ubrelvy)
medicines.use: Treats acute migraine attacks
medicines.precautions: Does not cause blood vessel constriction like triptans; safe for cardiovascular patients

medicines.name: Rimegepant (Nurtec ODT)
medicines.use: Treats acute migraine attacks and prevents them
medicines.dosage_guidance: 75mg orally dissolving tablet as needed or every other day
medicines.precautions: Orally disintegrating tablet works quickly; well-tolerated with less nausea than triptans

medicines.name: Erenumab (Aimovig)
medicines.use: Prevents migraine attacks
medicines.precautions: Can cause significant constipation and high blood pressure

medicines.name: Galcanezumab (Emgality)
medicines.use: Prevents migraine attacks and cluster headaches
medicines.precautions: Injection site reactions are common; very effective for episodic and chronic migraines

medicines.name: Fremanezumab (Ajovy)
medicines.use: Prevents migraine attacks
medicines.precautions: Flexible dosing schedule; injection site pain and redness are common

medicines.name: Botulinum Toxin Type A (Botox)
medicines.use: Prevents chronic migraines (>15 headache days/month)
medicines.dosage_guidance: Multiple injections in the head and neck every 12 weeks
medicines.precautions: Can cause temporary neck pain or muscle weakness/drooping

medicines.name: Nortriptyline (Pamelor)
medicines.use: Prevents migraines and treats nerve pain/depression
medicines.precautions: Similar to amitriptyline but generally better tolerated with fewer anticholinergic effects

medicines.name: Verapamil (Calan)
medicines.use: Prevents cluster headaches and migraines
medicines.precautions: Can cause severe constipation and slow heart rate; requires EKG monitoring at high doses

medicines.name: Prednisone (Deltasone)
medicines.use: Treats severe inflammation, asthma flares, and autoimmune conditions
medicines.dosage_guidance: Varies; often given as a 5-day taper pack
medicines.precautions: Must taper off to prevent adrenal crisis; causes weight gain, fluid retention, and mood changes

medicines.name: Methylprednisolone (Medrol)
medicines.use: Treats severe inflammation and allergic reactions
medicines.dosage_guidance: Often given as a 'Medrol Dosepak' 6-day taper
medicines.precautions: Take with food to prevent severe stomach upset; can increase blood pressure and blood sugar

medicines.name: Hydrocortisone (Cortef)
medicines.use: Replacement therapy for adrenal insufficiency (Addison's disease)
medicines.precautions: Must double or triple dose during illness or stress to prevent life-threatening adrenal crisis

medicines.name: Fludrocortisone (Florinef)
medicines.use: Replacement therapy for Addison's disease; treats low blood pressure
medicines.precautions: Causes significant salt and water retention; monitor potassium levels as they can drop
